title: Jenkins Segment 05
summary: A medium-difficulty Jenkins development segment covering 46 PRs across 7 dependency layers. Changes focus on core model refinements (Run, Cause, DirectoryBrowserSupport, CloudSet, SlaveComputer), security improvements (CSRF crumb issuer, CSP handling, HudsonPrivateSecurityRealm, AccessDeniedException3 serialization), CLI enhancements (BuildCommand), admin monitor UX (client-side redirects), frontend JavaScript updates, and build/infrastructure maintenance including LTS backports.
instructionPreview: `/workspace/requirements/` contains every requirement as a separate file. Each file describes one change request. Read them all, implement every one. Order does not matter, but some changes depend on others.
instruction:
You are an autonomous engineer. The repository at /workspace is at an earlier state of a real-world project.

`/workspace/requirements/` contains every requirement as a separate file. Each file describes one change request. Read them all, implement every one. Order does not matter, but some changes depend on others.

moduleDag.description: A medium-difficulty Jenkins development segment covering 46 PRs across 7 dependency layers. Changes focus on core model refinements (Run, Cause, DirectoryBrowserSupport, CloudSet, SlaveComputer), security improvements (CSRF crumb issuer, CSP handling, HudsonPrivateSecurityRealm, AccessDeniedException3 serialization), CLI enhancements (BuildCommand), admin monitor UX (client-side redirects), frontend JavaScript updates, and build/infrastructure maintenance including LTS backports.

nodes.label: Core Java (Server Logic)
nodes.description: Server-side Java: hudson/model (Run, Cause, DirectoryBrowserSupport, Computer, UpdateSite, Queue, BuildableItem), hudson/security (CSRF DefaultCrumbIssuer, HudsonPrivateSecurityRealm), jenkins/model (Jenkins, ModelObjectWithContextMenu, CloudSet), jenkins/util (JSONSignatureValidator, ClientHttpRedirect, HttpResponses), hudson/cli (BuildCommand), hudson/slaves (SlaveComputer), and admin monitors. 177 unique files with deep cross-cutting changes.

nodes.label: Core Resources (Views & Properties)
nodes.description: Jelly views, message bundles, and help files: AbstractBuild views (32 files), Run views (30 files), CSRF configuration UI, security API token pages, CloudSet index, admin monitor messages, and embedded JavaScript resources (BuildButtonColumn, project configurable, settings-subpage). Reflects UI/UX changes from server logic.

nodes.label: Frontend JavaScript
nodes.description: Client-side JS modules: dropdowns (combo-box, autocomplete, jumplists), add-item page logic, and utility helpers. Updates to interactive form components and navigation aids.

nodes.label: Frontend SCSS
nodes.description: Stylesheet updates for components and page layouts. Includes stylelintrc configuration and design system refinements for the segment's UI changes.

nodes.label: Build & Infrastructure
nodes.description: Maven configuration (pom.xml, bom/pom.xml), .mvn/maven.config, eslint config, ath.sh (acceptance test harness), packaging-ref.txt, Jenkinsfile, .gitpod configuration, and .stylelintrc. Controls CI, dependencies, and development environment setup.

nodes.label: WAR Module
nodes.description: WAR packaging: pom.xml, webapp resources (hudson-behavior.js with 21 edge references), executable/Main.java (Java version checks), and WAR-level build configuration. Bundles core into the deployable web application.

nodes.label: CLI Module
nodes.description: Jenkins CLI client: BuildCommand (8 PRs touching it), pom.xml, and supporting Java sources. Provides command-line access to Jenkins build operations.

edges.label: Jelly views render Java model objects and bind to server actions
WAR packages core Java and serves hudson-behavior.js APIs
CLI commands invoke core Java APIs (BuildCommand triggers builds)
embedded JS in resources interacts with frontend components
JS components reference CSS classes defined in SCSS
Maven BOM and root POM govern WAR dependency versions
POM manages core module build lifecycle and dependencies
frontend dropdowns/forms call server-side model APIs
